req.title: Create medication request (boundary)
req.statement: A prescribing clinician shall issue a MedicationRequest; requests for controlled substances shall require schedule verification. Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-033 mandates that a prescribing clinician can create a MedicationRequest, that controlled-substance (CII) requests are gated behind schedule verification, and that boundary conditions on timestamps and numeric fields (e.g. quantity, days-supply) are exercised. Four tests are designed: (1) EP positive – valid non-controlled prescription succeeds; (2) NEG – CII request without schedule verification returns 403; (3) BVA – quantity at the minimum boundary (1 unit) succeeds; (4) BVA – authoredOn timestamp at the exact RFC-3339 boundary (no sub-seconds) is accepted. Together these cover the happy path, the error pathway, and two boundary dimensions called out in the requirement.

tests.name: test_create_medication_request_non_controlled_success
preconditions: Caller is authenticated as a user with role=prescriber
Target medication is non-controlled (Schedule V or unscheduled)
actions: Build a valid FHIR MedicationRequest payload for a non-controlled drug
POST to /MedicationRequest with a valid prescriber bearer token
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains an id field
Pharmacy notification side-effect is triggered (status field or Bundle entry present)

tests.name: test_create_cii_medication_request_without_schedule_verification_returns_403
preconditions: Caller is authenticated as a user with role=prescriber
Target medication is a CII controlled substance (e.g. Oxycodone)
No schedule verification claim is present in the token or request
actions: Build a FHIR MedicationRequest payload for a CII controlled substance
POST to /MedicationRequest with a prescriber token that lacks schedule-verification
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains an error indicating schedule verification is required
trace: R-HLT-033: requests for controlled substances shall require schedule verification
R-HLT-033: error pathway – 403 without schedule verification for CII
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://fhir-lite/fhir/R4"

@pytest.fixture
def prescriber_token_no_schedule():
    return <fixture: get_prescriber_token_without_schedule_verification>

def test_create_cii_medication_request_without_schedule_verification_returns_403(
        prescriber_token_no_schedule):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "MedicationRequest",
        "status": "active",
        "intent": "order",
        "medicationCodeableConcept": {
            "coding": [{"system": "http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm",
                        "code": "1049502", "display": "Oxycodone 5 MG (CII)"}]
        },
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/test-patient-001"},
        "authoredOn": "2024-06-01T08:00:00Z",
        "requester": {"reference": "Practitioner/test-prescriber-001"},
        "dispenseRequest": {"quantity": {"value": 10, "unit": "tablet"}},
        "extension": [{"url": "http://fhir-lite/ext/controlled-schedule",
                       "valueCode": "CII"}]
    }
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {prescriber_token_no_schedule}",
               "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    resp = requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/MedicationRequest", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 403
    body = resp.json()
    assert <helper_fn: has_error_detail>(body, "schedule verification")

tests.name: test_create_medication_request_quantity_at_minimum_boundary
preconditions: Caller is authenticated as a user with role=prescriber
actions: Build a FHIR MedicationRequest payload with quantity set to 1 (minimum valid value)
POST to /MedicationRequest with a valid prescriber bearer token
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: HTTP 201 Created (quantity=1 is accepted as the minimum boundary)
trace: R-HLT-033: boundary cases on numeric elements must be tested

tests.name: test_create_medication_request_authored_on_exact_rfc3339_no_subseconds
preconditions: Caller is authenticated as a user with role=prescriber
actions: Build a FHIR MedicationRequest payload with authoredOn as a bare RFC-3339 datetime with no sub-second precision (boundary: minimum valid timestamp resolution)
POST to /MedicationRequest with a valid prescriber bearer token
Capture HTTP response status and body
Re-fetch resource by returned id and verify stored authoredOn
expected: Stored authoredOn matches or normalises to the submitted RFC-3339 value
No validation error relating to timestamp format
trace: R-HLT-033: boundary cases on timestamps must be tested
